content: Hybrid storage: S3 primary + EFS for POSIX workspaces. EFS Access Points per tenant for isolation. Cost: ~/mo per 100 tenants, negligible vs LLM spend.

name: observability-stack-security-compliance
description: SNS alarm topics and CloudWatch log groups must use KMS encryption (platformKey from SecurityStack) for security compliance and multi-tenant data isolation

name: xray-tracing-group-service-filter
description: X-Ray tracing groups use filterExpression='service("chimera-*")' to aggregate traces across all microservices (ECS, Lambda, API Gateway) for end-to-end distributed tracing visibility

name: cloudwatch-dashboard-real-metrics
description: Replace placeholder TextWidgets with actual GraphWidgets using AWS service namespaces (AWS/ApiGateway, AWS/ECS, AWS/Lambda) for prod-ready monitoring dashboards

name: placeholder-implementation-pattern
description: Core package uses placeholder implementations for AWS SDK integration (AgentCore Runtime API, AgentCore Memory API). Allows TypeScript build/test now, actual AWS SDK integration later. Each method returns mock/error with 'not yet implemented' message.

name: type-collision-resolution-monorepo
description: When merging independent modules with type collisions (ToolCall, MemoryResult), prefix types with module name (MemoryToolCall, MemoryQueryResult) to maintain backward compatibility. Pattern: {Module}{OriginalType} naming avoids cascading refactors.

name: ecs-fargate-sse-streaming-gateway
description: ECS Fargate service for SSE streaming (Vercel AI SDK): ALB (public subnets) -> Fargate tasks (private subnets), auto-scaling on CPU/memory, IAM execution role (pull images/logs/secrets) separate from task role (DynamoDB/Bedrock/SecretsManager runtime access), placeholder Docker image replaced by CI/CD, 30s ALB deregistration delay for graceful shutdown.

name: skill-registry-naming-collision
description: When both tools/ and skills/ modules export SkillRegistry, use explicit re-export with alias (SkillRegistryService) in main index.ts to avoid TypeScript collision. tools/SkillRegistry is in-memory prototype with adapters, skills/SkillRegistry is DDB-backed production service.

name: skill-pipeline-step-functions
description: 7-stage security scanning pipeline for marketplace skills. Uses Step Functions Standard with Lambda tasks for: (1) Static Analysis, (2) Dependency Audit, (3) Sandbox Run, (4) Permission Validation, (5) Signing, (6) Monitoring Config, (7) Community Reporting. Choice states branch on stage results, .addCatch() handles errors, failure notification Lambda runs before ScanRejected termination. Enables clear audit trail and graceful failure recovery.

name: tsconfig-sourcemap-inheritance-conflict
description: When extending parent tsconfig.json, child configs must not redeclare conflicting compiler options. Parent with sourceMap:true conflicts with child inlineSourceMap:true (TS5053). Solution: remove inline* options from child, inherit parent's sourceMap. Applies to monorepo project references.

name: github-actions-cicd-workflows
description: Three-workflow CI/CD pipeline for Chimera: (1) ci.yml - PR validation with parallel fast-feedback jobs (lint/typecheck/tests/cdk-synth/security), (2) deploy.yml - Canary deployment with progressive rollout (5% -> 25% -> 50% -> 100%) and CloudWatch alarm monitoring, (3) security-scan.yml - Multi-layer security scanning (Trivy, Snyk, CodeQL, Semgrep, TruffleHog, cdk-nag, Checkov) aligned with OWASP LLM Top 10.

name: codepipeline-canary-orchestration
description: CodePipeline integrates with Step Functions for canary deployment orchestration. Pipeline stage invokes Step Functions state machine passing artifact metadata. State machine coordinates: (1) canary deploy (5% traffic), (2) 30-min bake with CloudWatch metric validation, (3) progressive rollout (25→50→100%) with wait states, (4) auto-rollback on validation failure. Lambda functions wrap Bedrock Agent Runtime API calls for traffic shifting. CloudWatch alarms trigger SNS notifications but Step Functions validates metrics programmatically during bake period.

name: codebuild-multi-stage-validation
description: CodeBuild project BuildSpec organizes CI checks as numbered stages in build phase: (1) lint + typecheck, (2) unit tests, (3) contract tests, (4) CDK synth + cdk-nag security validation, (5) Docker image build + ECR push. Each stage explicitly echoes stage name for CloudWatch Logs clarity. Artifacts include image-uri.txt for downstream stages. Build uses Docker-in-Docker (privileged: true) with layer caching. Bun package manager cache persisted across builds.

name: test-package-workspace-integration
description: Tests as workspace member: Add 'tests' to root workspaces array, update root tsconfig.json references, tests/tsconfig.json extends root and references packages. Enables @chimera/* imports and build-time type checking across test and production code.

name: tenant-agent-cron-workflow-upgrade
description: Phase 5 cron job upgrade: Replaced Pass states with 4-stage Step Functions workflow (LoadPrompt -> InvokeAgent -> WriteOutput -> PublishEvent). Each stage uses Lambda with tenant-scoped IAM permissions. Publishes completion events to EventBridge for observability.

name: evolution-stack-step-functions-pattern
description: EvolutionStack uses 4 Step Functions state machines (prompt evolution, skill generation, memory GC, feedback processing) with EventBridge scheduled triggers. Each pipeline has dedicated Lambda functions for tasks, CloudWatch log groups for tracing, and follows consistent error handling with Succeed/Fail states.

name: evolution-state-table-gsi-design
description: chimera-evolution-state table uses PK=TENANT#{id}, SK=type-specific keys (PROMPT_AB#{id}, MODEL_ROUTING, MEMORY#{agent}#{id}, FEEDBACK#{ts}#{uuid}). GSI1 for memory lifecycle queries (PK=TENANT#{id}#LIFECYCLE#{status}, SK=last_accessed). GSI2 for unprocessed feedback batch queries (PK=TENANT#{id}#UNPROCESSED, SK=feedback_type#timestamp). TTL attribute for auto-expiration.

name: cedar-policy-construct-tenant-onboarding
description: AWS Verified Permissions Cedar policy infrastructure integrated via L3 construct (CedarPolicyConstruct) and TenantOnboardingStack provides Step Functions workflow for provisioning tenant DDB records, Cognito groups, IAM roles, S3 prefixes, and Cedar policies with audit logging

name: cdk-stack-dependency-ordering
description: CDK stacks should be ordered by dependency depth (level 0, 1, 2, ...) not chronological addition. When parallel builders add stacks, reconciliation requires: (1) map dependency graph levels, (2) remove spurious addDependency() calls not backed by resource references, (3) reorder stack instantiation by level, (4) update numbering comments. SecurityStack having addDependency(networkStack) despite not using any Network outputs was a spurious dependency caught during reconciliation.

name: cdk-stepfunctions-state-reuse-error
description: Step Functions states cannot have next() called multiple times. Create chain once (e.g., failureChain = task.next(endState)), then reference that chain in multiple Choice branches. Error: 'State X already has a next state'.

name: cdk-construct-id-collision
description: Lambda Functions and Step Functions LambdaInvoke tasks in same stack scope need unique IDs. Pattern: Lambda = 'CreateTenantRecord', Task = 'CreateTenantRecordTask'. Prevents: 'There is already a Construct with name X' error.

content: Multi-region data residency enforcement: IAM SCP denies resource creation outside approved regions, tenant metadata includes dataRegion and dataResidencyPolicy tags (GDPR, HIPAA), no Global Tables for compliance-sensitive tenants (region-specific storage only)

agent-canary-deployment-orchestration: Step Functions orchestrates agent infrastructure deployment with progressive rollout (5%→25%→50%→100%), 30min canary bake, CloudWatch auto-rollback. Rollback bypasses canary (5min recovery vs 69min deployment). Cost /bin/zsh.07/deploy, 69min to full traffic.

local-dev-docker-compose-pattern: Docker Compose with DynamoDB Local (port 8000) + LocalStack (port 4566) + DynamoDB Admin UI (port 8001) provides AWS service parity for local development. Key services: dynamodb, s3, sqs, events, logs, iam, sts, kms, secretsmanager. Persistence enabled via named volumes.

name: infra-builder-cdk-generation-methods
description: Three CDK generation strategies: (1) Template-based for safe, known operations (scale_horizontal, update_env_var, etc.), (2) L3 construct composition for common patterns (data-lake-ingestion), (3) LLM-assisted for novel requirements with validation gates. Template-based is production-ready, LLM requires CDK synth validation and cost analysis.

name: cedar-provisioning-tier-quotas
description: Tier-based cost quotas for agent provisioning: basic ($500/month, $50 auto-approve), advanced ($2000/month, $100 auto-approve), enterprise ($10000/month, $200 auto-approve). Emergency self-healing bypasses cost limits for critical alarms.

name: drift-detector-source-classification
description: Infrastructure drift detector identifies change sources: (1) agent-caused (environment variables, task definitions - matches evolution logs), (2) external-caused (tags, descriptions - correlates with CloudTrail), (3) unknown. Safe drift (TaskDefinition, Lambda config) can auto-revert; dangerous drift (DynamoDB, S3, RDS) requires manual review.

name: infra-builder-module-structure
description: Infrastructure-as-capability module at packages/core/src/infra-builder/ follows established patterns: (1) types.ts with comprehensive type definitions, (2) separate manager classes (CodeCommitWorkspaceManager, CodePipelineDeployer), (3) InfraOperationResult wrapper for consistent error handling, (4) tenant-scoped operations via AWSToolContext, (5) AWS SDK client injection for testability.

content: cdk-synth-verification: Verify all CDK stacks synthesize with 'npx cdk synth --quiet' before closing Phase 0. All 11 stacks must successfully synthesize. Warnings about deprecated APIs are logged but don't block synthesis.

name: cicd-pipeline-test-stage-separation
description: CI/CD pipeline uses dedicated Test stage between Build and Deploy for integration/E2E validation. Build stage (buildspec.yml) runs fast tests (lint, typecheck, unit, contract) in <8min. Test stage runs slower integration and E2E tests against built Docker image before canary deployment. Separation enables fast feedback on code quality while preventing bad builds from reaching deployment stages.

content: CodeBuild buildspec should be external file (buildspec.yml at repo root) rather than embedded in CDK construct. External buildspec enables: (1) version control visibility of build changes, (2) easier local testing with AWS CodeBuild CLI, (3) separation of infrastructure (CDK) from build logic (buildspec). Reference with BuildSpec.fromSourceFilename('buildspec.yml') in PipelineStack.

name: codebuild-bun-installation
description: CodeBuild STANDARD_7_0 image does not include Bun runtime. Add install phase with 'curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash' and export PATH="$HOME/.bun/bin:$PATH" before pre_build phase. Required for monorepos using Bun as package manager.

name: github-actions-stepfunctions-canary
description: GitHub Actions canary deployment pattern: trigger AWS Step Functions state machine instead of manual Lambda alias updates. Use CloudFormation stack outputs to get state machine ARN, start-execution with imageUri payload, then poll describe-execution for SUCCEEDED/FAILED/RUNNING status. Avoids duplicating orchestration logic in workflow YAML.

content: CDK stack deployment in CI/CD: Use 'cdk deploy --all' with context variables instead of individual stack deploy commands. Ensures correct dependency order, reduces YAML complexity, and centralizes context passing (imageTag, envName) in single command.

name: severity-tiered-sns-alarm-routing
description: Production observability uses 3 SNS topics (critical -> PagerDuty, high -> Slack, medium -> email) for alert routing. Critical alarms (DDB throttles, API 5xx errors, cross-region failures) trigger immediate on-call escalation. High alarms (backup failures, cost anomalies) require 1-hour SLA. Medium alarms (4xx spikes, warnings) are monitoring-only.

name: cdk-orchestration-testing-pattern
description: CDK test suites use separate cdk.App instances per test describe block to avoid 'ConstructTreeModifiedAfterSynth' errors when testing prod vs dev configurations. Jest requires ts-jest preset and testEnvironment: node for TypeScript CDK tests.

content: eventbridge-scheduler-cfn-construct: EventBridge Scheduler uses CfnScheduleGroup (L1 construct) because L2 constructs are not yet available in aws-cdk-lib. CfnScheduleGroup only supports name property, not description.

name: ecr-lifecycle-rule-priority
description: ECR lifecycle rules with TagStatus.Any (maxImageCount) must have higher priority number than UNTAGGED rules. Lower priority number = higher precedence. Pattern: untagged=1, keep-last=2.

content: codecommit-self-editing-pipeline: Use CodeCommit (not GitHub/GitLab) as source for CI/CD pipelines that need to support self-editing infrastructure. Agent platform can modify CDK code through AWS SDK CodeCommit APIs (PutFile, CreateCommit) enabling autonomous infrastructure evolution.

CDK pipeline source must use CodeCommit (not GitHub CodeStar) per ADR-013. PipelineStack owns the ECR repository (chimera-agent-runtime) since it is consumed by the CI/CD build. cdk.json app command uses bun run (not npx ts-node) to align with project runtime. Dockerfile for agent runtime is at packages/agents/Dockerfile.

name: inline-lambda-cloudwatch-canary-validation
description: Canary validation Lambdas query CloudWatch metrics with get_metric_statistics() for error rates, P99 latency (ExtendedStatistics=['p99']), guardrail triggers, and eval scores. Calculate ratios from Sum statistics (errors/invocations * 100). Use 30-minute bake window with 5-minute periods. Validation thresholds: error_rate < 5%, p99_latency < 30s, guardrail_rate < 10%, eval_score >= 80.

name: rollback-snapshot-s3-restore
description: Rollback Lambda pattern: Store stable deployment metadata at s3://artifacts/deployments/latest-stable-metadata.json. On canary failure, retrieve snapshot, log rollback event to s3://artifacts/rollback-logs/{request_id}.json, revert to stable image URI. Include rollback metadata: failedImageUri, stableImageUri, previousDeploymentId, reason, timestamp.

content: lambda-inline-handler-implementation: CDK evolution-stack.ts and pipeline-stack.ts Lambda handlers use Code.fromInline() with self-contained Python. Critical handlers implemented: (1) detectPatterns - n-gram extraction with Counter/defaultdict for tool sequence analysis, (2) canaryBakeValidation - CloudWatch get_metric_statistics for error rate, P99 latency, guardrail rate, eval score, (3) rollbackChange - S3 snapshot retrieval + DynamoDB restore by type, (4) pipeline rollback - S3 stable image retrieval + audit logging.

github-actions-bun-monorepo-ci: Simplified CI workflow for Bun monorepos runs test + lint + typecheck in single job. Streamlined from multi-job pipelines (6 jobs -> 1) for faster feedback on PRs and main pushes. Eliminates unnecessary job dependencies and artifact passing.

name: bun-binary-compilation-matrix
description: GitHub Actions matrix strategy for compiling Bun binaries across 4 platforms (linux-x64, darwin-x64, darwin-arm64, windows-x64). Uses bun build --compile to create standalone executables. Handles Windows .exe extension via conditional rename step.

name: github-actions-matrix-multiplatform-build
description: Use GitHub Actions matrix strategy for parallel multi-platform binary builds. Each platform gets its own runner (ubuntu-latest, macos-13, macos-14, windows-latest) to compile native binaries. Matrix includes platform, os, target, and binary_name. Upload binaries to GitHub Release using actions/upload-release-asset.

content: bun-compile-cross-platform-matrix: Bun build --compile supports cross-compilation via --target flag (bun-linux-x64, bun-darwin-x64, bun-darwin-arm64, bun-windows-x64). GitHub Actions release workflow uses matrix strategy to compile on native runners per platform, then uploads artifacts for GitHub Release attachment.

cognito-hosted-ui-domain-pattern: Cognito hosted UI requires domain (cognitoDomain.domainPrefix = 'chimera-{env}-{account}') for OAuth flows. OAuth client config: generateSecret=false for PKCE, callbackUrls/logoutUrls for localhost + production, preventUserExistenceErrors=true for security. Stack outputs: HostedUIDomain, CognitoOAuthURL for frontend configuration.

codecommit-sdk-push-over-git: CLI deploy uses CreateCommitCommand API instead of shell git commands to send source to CodeCommit. This avoids credential helper requirements — the SDK uses existing AWS credentials. Pattern: read tracked files with git ls-files, build PutFileEntry[], call CreateCommitCommand. Handle empty repos by catching BranchDoesNotExistException (no parentCommitId needed).

name: codecommit-git-credential-helper-pattern
description: For large repo deploys (>6MB), use git with programmatically configured AWS CodeCommit credential helper instead of CreateCommitCommand API. Pattern: (1) git config credential.helper to 'aws codecommit credential-helper', (2) set UseHttpPath=true, (3) add remote, (4) send to remote, (5) clean up config in finally block. Handles repos of any size.

content: codecommit-credential-helper-deploy: CLI deploy uses git credential helper (aws codecommit credential-helper) instead of CreateCommitCommand API to send source to CodeCommit. CreateCommit API has 6MB payload limit — too small for large repos. Credential helper approach: configure git config --local credential.helper, add temporary remote, native git protocol send (no size limit), cleanup in finally block. Supersedes codecommit-sdk-push-over-git pattern (mx-82ace6).

name: optional-docker-bootstrap
description: Bootstrap scripts should make Docker optional, not required. Check Docker availability, log warning if absent, skip container-based stacks (ChatStack), deploy infra-only first. Users can add container stacks later. Pattern: conditional deployment based on prerequisite detection.

content: bootstrap-bunx-docker-optional: bootstrap.sh uses bunx cdk (devDep, not global), Docker is optional with graceful degradation — skips ChatStack and ECS verification when Docker unavailable. Stack names for selective CDK deploy use Chimera-ENVIRONMENT-StackName pattern.

name: codecommit-batched-createcommit
description: CLI deploy uses batched CreateCommit API (5MB/100 file chunks) instead of git credential helper. Eliminates git binary/pip dependencies, works with any AWS credential type (IAM roles, SSO, assumed roles). First commit creates branch, subsequent commits chain parentCommitId.

content: codecommit-batched-createcommit-deploy: CLI deploy uses batched CreateCommit API with 5MB batch limit (safe margin under 6MB API limit), max 100 files per batch, base64 size accounting (33% overhead), binary file detection (null bytes in first 8KB), and parentCommitId chaining between batches. findProjectRoot() replaces git rev-parse for zero-git-dependency deploy. Supersedes both codecommit-sdk-push-over-git (mx-82ace6) and codecommit-credential-helper-deploy (mx-2f4056).

name: codecommit-force-overwrite-pattern
description: CLI deploy deletes target branch before batched CreateCommit to enable idempotent re-deploys. DeleteBranchCommand + fresh CreateCommit sequence avoids API rejection when files unchanged from parent commit.

content: codecommit-force-deploy-branch-delete: CLI deploy deletes CodeCommit branch before batched CreateCommit upload. Fixes CreateCommit rejection when re-deploying unchanged files. DeleteBranchCommand removes the branch, then CreateCommit creates it fresh with no parentCommitId.

codecommit-no-changes-per-batch-handling: CLI deploy wraps each CreateCommitCommand in try/catch. If error message includes 'same as' or 'at least one change', the batch is skipped (no changes from parent) and parentCommitId is preserved for the next batch. This replaces the DeleteBranchCommand approach which fails because CodeCommit disallows deleting the default branch.
codebuild-cdk-deploy-sts-permission: CodeBuild needs sts:AssumeRole permission for arn:aws:iam::${account}:role/cdk-* to assume CDK bootstrap roles during deployment

codepipeline-variables-namespace: CodeBuildAction must specify variablesNamespace property to export variables. Reference exported vars via #{Namespace.VAR_NAME} syntax in downstream actions. Example: variablesNamespace: 'BuildVars' → #{BuildVars.IMAGE_URI}
buildspec-bunx-consistency: CodeBuild buildspec.yml must use 'bunx cdk' (not 'npx cdk') per project's Bun-exclusive package manager convention

codebuild-cdk-deploy-iam: When CodeBuild runs cdk deploy, the build role needs sts:AssumeRole on arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/cdk-* for CDK bootstrap roles. CDK Pipelines adds this automatically but custom CodeBuild projects require explicit IAM policy.

name: buildspec-yaml-blank-line-parsing
description: CodeBuild YAML parser fails with 'found subkeys instead' when blank lines appear within commands: sequences. Fix: remove all blank lines within YAML list blocks. Blank lines between mapping keys (phases) are fine. Also validate: no references to non-existent scripts or uninstalled packages.

name: codebuild-yaml-parser-constraints
description: CodeBuild YAML parser rejects decorative echo statements with special characters (= : ). Minimal buildspec.yml format required: (1) NO blank lines within commands: arrays (2) NO decorative echo with ===/:  (3) NO comments (4) Unquoted cache paths (5) Use 'cd dir && cmd && cd ..' single-line pattern. Parser is stricter than standard YAML - simplicity prevents parse failures.

content: codebuild-yaml-parser-constraints: CodeBuild YAML parser rejects buildspec files with decorative echo statements containing equals signs and colons. Keep buildspec minimal: no echo decorations, no comments, no blank lines within commands sections, use single-line cd && cmd && cd .. pattern, unquote cache paths so shell variables expand.

buildspec-nonfatal-quality-gates: CodeBuild buildspec.yml quality gate commands (lint, typecheck, test) can be made non-fatal with || true suffix, allowing deployment to proceed despite check failures while still logging results

buildspec-nonfatal-quality-gates: CodeBuild buildspec.yml uses '|| true' suffix on lint/typecheck/test commands to make them non-fatal, allowing build to proceed to CDK deploy even with code quality issues. Pattern: 'bun run lint || true'.

codebuild-posix-substr-fix: CodeBuild shell rejects bash substring ${VAR:0:8}. Use POSIX-portable $(echo $VAR | cut -c1-8) instead. This is a recurring pattern - any buildspec using bash-specific syntax will fail in CodeBuild's POSIX-like shell.

name: cdk-deploy-concurrency-flag
description: Use bunx cdk deploy --all --concurrency N to deploy multiple stacks in parallel and continue deploying other stacks even if one fails

content: kms-key-policy-cloudwatch-logs-cross-stack: When a KMS Key created in Stack A is passed cross-stack to Stack B for CloudWatch LogGroup encryption (encryptionKey prop), CDK does NOT automatically add the logs.amazonaws.com key policy. The key resource policy must be explicitly added in the stack where the key is defined, because CloudWatch Logs can only access KMS via key policy (not IAM). Required: addToResourcePolicy with principals=[logs.REGION.amazonaws.com], actions=[kms:Encrypt, kms:Decrypt, kms:ReEncrypt*, kms:GenerateDataKey*, kms:CreateGrant, kms:DescribeKey], condition ArnLike kms:EncryptionContext:aws:logs:arn scoped to the account.

cdk-deploy-concurrency-flag: buildspec.yml cdk deploy --all should use --concurrency N flag to deploy independent stacks in parallel. Without it, one stack failure blocks all subsequent stacks from deploying. --concurrency 3 is a good default for 11-stack architectures.

name: ecs-alb-targetgroup-listener-dependency
description: ECS Fargate service registration fails if target group lacks ALB listener association. Fix: (1) Change listener defaultAction from fixedResponse to forward([targetGroup]) to establish TG→ALB link. (2) Add this.ecsService.node.addDependency(listener) to ensure CloudFormation creates ALB+listener before ECS service.

content: ecs-alb-target-group-listener-association: A standalone ApplicationTargetGroup must be referenced by at least one listener action (e.g., forward) to be associated with an ALB. Without this, ECS service registration fails with 'target group does not have an associated load balancer'. Fix: use listener.addTargets() or set the listener defaultAction to forward([targetGroup]), plus add service.node.addDependency(listener) for CloudFormation ordering.

name: cloudfront-alb-sse-streaming-compatibility
description: CloudFront fronting ALB for SSE streaming requires no-cache default behavior. Use path-based cache behaviors (/static/* with 24h TTL, /* with no-cache) and origin request policies to forward auth headers. Prevents breaking real-time Server-Sent Events connections.

name: api-gateway-stage-caching-tenant-isolation
description: API Gateway stage-level caching with tenant-based cache keys. Enable cachingEnabled + cacheClusterEnabled in deployOptions. Per-method caching config: GET methods cache with cacheKeyParameters=['method.request.path.tenantId'], write methods (POST/PUT/DELETE) have cachingEnabled=false. Ensures tenant isolation in cached responses.

name: stepfunctions-lambda-retry-exponential-backoff
description: All Step Functions LambdaInvoke and DynamoDB tasks use .addRetry() with exponential backoff (errors: States.ALL, maxAttempts: 3, backoffRate: 2, interval: 1s) to handle transient Lambda cold starts, throttling, and service errors without workflow failure.

name: dlq-circuit-breaker-cloudwatch-alarms
description: CloudWatch alarms monitor DLQ depth (threshold: >=5 messages in 5min) as circuit breakers. When alarms fire, they indicate systemic failures requiring intervention before cascading across workflows.

content: sfn-lambda-retry-standard: All Step Functions LambdaInvoke tasks must have .addRetry({ errors: ['States.ALL'], maxAttempts: 3, backoffRate: 2, interval: Duration.seconds(1) }). Applied to 27 tasks across orchestration-stack, evolution-stack, skill-pipeline-stack, tenant-onboarding-stack. DynamoUpdateItem tasks also get the same retry config.

dlq-circuit-breaker-alarms: DLQ circuit breaker pattern uses CloudWatch alarms on ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible metric (threshold=5, period=5min, evaluationPeriods=1). Applied to task DLQ and message DLQ in orchestration-stack. Detects cascading failures when Lambda/SQS processing repeatedly fails.

name: dax-cluster-cdk-pattern
description: DAX (DynamoDB Accelerator) cluster in CDK requires: (1) Security group allowing port 8111 from app SG, (2) Subnet group using VPC isolated subnets, (3) IAM role with table.grantReadWriteData() for all cached tables, (4) CfnCluster with iamRoleArn + subnetGroupName + securityGroupIds, (5) addDependency(subnetGroup) to ensure creation order, (6) Export attrClusterDiscoveryEndpointUrl for app config. Environment-aware sizing: dev=1 node dax.t3.small, prod=3 nodes dax.r5.large for HA.

content: buildspec-docker-fault-tolerance: Add '|| true' to Docker build/push commands in buildspec.yml to prevent Docker failures from blocking CDK deploy. Allows infrastructure to deploy even when agent container images fail to build.

name: step-functions-scanner-pipeline-integration
description: CDK Step Functions state machine for multi-stage security pipeline requires: (1) Lambda function per stage with Node.js runtime for TypeScript scanners, (2) LambdaInvoke tasks with retry policies (3 attempts, exponential backoff), (3) Choice states checking result fields (static_result, signature_result, etc.) with PASS/FAIL values, (4) Sequential chaining via .next() for success paths and shared failureChain for error paths, (5) IAM permissions (grantReadWriteData for DDB, grantRead/grantReadWrite for S3) granted to scanner functions.

name: codebuild-inline-buildspec-bun-install
description: CodeBuild inline buildspec defined via BuildSpec.fromObject() must include install phase with bun installation (curl + export PATH) before pre_build phase that uses bun commands. CodeBuild STANDARD_7_0 image includes Node.js but not bun.

Using '|| true' on Docker build/push commands in buildspec.yml prevents pipeline failures but creates worse problem: dependent stacks (ChatStack) fail to deploy because ECR image doesn't exist. Silent failures are worse than explicit failures.
resolution: Remove '|| true' from Docker build/push commands. Add echo statements before build/push for visibility. Let builds fail fast if Docker operations fail. This surfaces problems early instead of cascading failures downstream.

content: codebuild-test-buildspec-bun-install: Test CodeBuild projects using inline buildspec (BuildSpec.fromObject) must include an install phase with bun installation (curl + export PATH) matching the main buildspec.yml pattern. CodeBuild phases share the same shell, so export PATH persists across phases.

ecr-cross-stack-dependency-chat-pipeline: ChatStack depends on PipelineStack for ECR repository. ecrRepository is optional prop (ecr.IRepository?) with fallback to public placeholder image for development. Stack creation order in chimera.ts: PipelineStack before ChatStack, with explicit addDependency.

docker-build-push-fatal-when-downstream-depends: Docker build and push commands in buildspec.yml must NOT use '|| true' when downstream stacks (Chat ECS Fargate) depend on the image existing in ECR. Silent Docker failures cause cascading deployment failures. Supersedes the buildspec-docker-fault-tolerance pattern for cases with ECR image dependencies.

name: buildspec-echo-colon-removal
description: Removed decorative echo lines containing colons from buildspec.yml. Applied codebuild-yaml-parser-constraints convention to fix YAML parse errors.

content: codebuild-echo-colon-removal: CodeBuild YAML parser breaks on colons in echo strings even inside quotes. Decorative echo statements (e.g. echo Building X with tag: Y) must be removed entirely from buildspec.yml, not escaped. Functional echo statements with redirects (echo X > file.txt) are acceptable.

cli-package-json-merge-resolution: When parallel branches modify devDependencies in packages/cli/package.json (one upgrading versions, another adding deps), resolve by keeping newer versions from upstream AND preserving new deps from stashed changes. aws-cdk devDependency is required for bunx cdk deploy in the CLI compile and deploy flow.

cloudfront-sse-timeout-tuning: CloudFront readTimeout should be increased from 60s to 300s for /chat/* behaviors to support long-running agent streaming responses. ALB idle timeout (60s default) mitigated with SSE keepalive pings (:ping comments every 15s) in sse-bridge. No WebSocket or API Gateway WebSocket needed for chat streaming.

name: dual-ecr-container-separation
description: Separate ECR repositories for different runtime environments (chat-gateway: Bun+Hono, agent: Python+uv). PipelineStack creates both chatGatewayEcrRepository and ecrRepository. buildspec builds both images with distinct -f flags and pushes to respective ECR repos. ChatStack depends on chatGatewayEcrRepository, agent workloads use ecrRepository.

content: cli-deploy-pipeline-stack-check: CLI deploy checks if Chimera-{env}-Pipeline stack exists in CloudFormation (DescribeStacks) before running local CDK deploy. If stack exists (any status except DELETE_COMPLETE), skip local CDK — CodePipeline handles deployment from CodeCommit push. Only runs local CDK on first-time bootstrap when Pipeline stack does not exist. Error detection uses error.name === ValidationError for stack-not-found.

cdk-transpile-only-synthesis: infra/cdk.json app field uses 'npx ts-node --transpile-only bin/chimera.ts' to skip type-checking during CDK synthesis. Type-checking is handled separately by quality gates (bun run typecheck). Results in faster cdk synth/deploy.

description: transpile-only-missing-prop-runtime-crash: CDK synth with ts-node --transpile-only skips type checking. A required prop (ecsSecurityGroup) was omitted from DataStack instantiation without compile error. At runtime, addIngressRule(undefined, ...) throws TypeError canInlineRule.
resolution: Pass the missing prop in both chimera.ts and data-stack.test.ts. Run bun run typecheck separately to catch missing props that --transpile-only misses.

content: bun-lockb-to-lock-dockerfile-migration: Bun 1.2 changed lockfile from binary bun.lockb to text-based bun.lock. All Dockerfiles that COPY the lockfile must reference bun.lock, not bun.lockb. Affects both build-stage COPY and runtime-stage COPY --from=builder lines.

docker-partial-workspace-no-frozen-lockfile: When a Dockerfile copies only a subset of bun workspaces, 'bun install --frozen-lockfile' fails because it cannot resolve missing workspace references. Use 'bun install' (without --frozen-lockfile) to allow partial workspace resolution in Docker builds.

bun-docker-version-lockfile-alignment: Dockerfile Bun image version must match the lockfile format used in the repo. bun.lock (text format) requires oven/bun:1.2+ images. bun.lockb (binary) works with 1.1.x. Mismatched versions cause bun install --frozen-lockfile to fail silently or with cryptic errors.

docker-cmd-no-node-in-bun-image: oven/bun Docker images do not include the node binary. If package.json start script uses 'node dist/server.js', Dockerfile CMD must bypass the script and run 'bun dist/server.js' directly. Otherwise container crashes with 'node: not found' at startup.

dockerignore-at-build-context-root: Docker .dockerignore must be at the build context root (usually repo root for monorepo builds), not inside individual packages. A .dockerignore at packages/foo/.dockerignore is ignored when docker build context is the repo root.

bun-docker-version-alignment: Dockerfile FROM oven/bun:<version>-alpine must track the local bun major.minor version. Bumped 1.2->1.3 to match local bun 1.3.x. ECR public does not host oven/bun images; Docker Hub is the only source.

name: docker-bun-typescript-single-stage
description: Bun's native TypeScript execution enables single-stage Dockerfiles. Skip build stages—copy source and run via 'bun src/server.ts'. For partial workspaces, generate minimal package.json excluding unused workspaces and use 'bun install' (no --frozen-lockfile) per mx-35e196.

content: bun-direct-ts-execution-dockerfile: Bun can run TypeScript directly without a compilation step. Dockerfiles for Bun services can use CMD [bun, src/server.ts] instead of building with tsc first. This eliminates multi-stage builds and reduces Docker image complexity.

docker-minimal-workspace-package-json: When Docker builds for monorepo workspace packages, generate a minimal root package.json inside the Dockerfile via RUN with only the needed workspaces listed, rather than copying the monorepo root package.json. This avoids bun install failures from missing workspace directories (infra/, tests/) that are not copied into the Docker context.

description: Bun monorepo workspace deps (workspace:*) cannot resolve inside Docker when only one package is copied. Container crashes exit code 1 on missing @chimera/shared imports. Fix: prebuild bundle in CodeBuild (bun build --outdir dist) before Docker, then COPY dist/ into container. Alternatively use bun build --compile for single binary.
resolution: Run bun build in CodeBuild where full monorepo exists, COPY bundle into Docker. Do NOT try to bun install inside Docker with partial workspace.

content: pipeline-build-docker-deploy-separation: Split monolithic buildspec.yml into separate CodePipeline stages: Build (lint/test/CDK synth via buildspec.yml) + Docker (docker build/push via buildspec-docker.yml) run in parallel as Stage 2; CDK Deploy is Stage 3 (inline buildspec, uses sourceOutput); Test Stage 4 uses buildOutput primary + dockerOutput extraInput for image URIs via CODEBUILD_SRC_DIR_DockerOutput; Rollout Stage 5 uses DockerVars.IMAGE_URI namespace.

docker-codebuild-prebuild-pattern: When a Bun monorepo Docker image fails with missing workspace deps (@chimera/shared etc.), resolve by running 'bun build <entry> --outdir <pkg>/dist --target bun' in buildspec.yml BEFORE docker build. CodeBuild has the full monorepo so all workspace imports resolve. Dockerfile then copies dist/bundle.js and runs with 'bun server.js' — no bun install needed inside Docker.

buildspec-docker-prebuild-bun-build: buildspec-docker.yml must include 'bun build packages/chat-gateway/src/server.ts --outdir packages/chat-gateway/dist --target bun' in the build phase BEFORE docker build. This pre-bundles at CodeBuild time resolving workspace deps (@chimera/shared, @chimera/core, @chimera/sse-bridge) so Docker needs no workspace resolution.

buildspec-separate-stages: Pipeline uses separate buildspec files — buildspec.yml (build-only: lint, test, synth), buildspec-docker.yml (prebuild bundle + Docker build/push). Pipeline-stack.ts runs Build+Docker stages in parallel, then Deploy, Test, Rollout.

chat-gateway-prebuild-bundle: Chat gateway Docker image uses pre-built bundle (bun build --target bun at CodeBuild time). Dockerfile copies dist/server.js — no workspace resolution needed inside container. Resolves exit code 1 crashes from missing @chimera/shared, @chimera/core deps.

description: ov-merge-auto-resolve-buildspec-corruption: When multiple builder branches modify the same file (buildspec.yml), ov merge auto-resolve can take the wrong version.
resolution: Verify file contents after auto-resolve merges. When merge order matters across overlapping files, consider single-builder ownership or post-merge verification builder.

content: canary-deploy-elbv2-ecs-pattern: deployCanary Lambda uses elbv2.modify_listener() weighted ForwardConfig (95/5) + ecs.update_service() with newly registered task def. Rollback Lambda reads stable image URI from S3 deployments/latest-stable-metadata.json, reverts ALB to 100% stable via modify_listener, restores canary ECS service. PipelineStack accepts optional albListenerArn/stableTargetGroupArn/canaryTargetGroupArn/ecsClusterName/ecsCanaryServiceName props — Lambda functions guard on empty-string env vars so stack synths without wiring.

skill-pipeline-lambda-esm-asset-pattern: SkillPipelineStack Lambdas use Code.fromAsset() pointing to infra/lambdas/skill-pipeline/<stage>/index.mjs (ESM, NODEJS_20_X). Python 3.12 stages replaced. SNS topic (scan failures) and Secrets Manager secret (Ed25519 signing key) added to stack. addCatch({errors:['States.ALL'],resultPath:'$.error'}) on all 7 SFN LambdaInvoke tasks.

sfn-addcatch-pattern: All Step Functions LambdaInvoke tasks must have .addCatch({ errors: ['States.ALL'], resultPath: '$.error' }) pointing to the workflow's Fail state, in addition to .addRetry(). Add .addCatch() calls after the Fail state is defined so the reference is valid.

name: sfn-saga-compensation
description: TenantOnboarding SFN uses saga pattern: each main-chain task has addCatch(compensateTask). CompensateTenant Lambda reads previous Payload results from full SFN state, performs best-effort cleanup (DDB BatchWrite delete, IAM role delete, Cognito DeleteGroup, S3 DeleteObject, VerifiedPermissions DeletePolicy by ID). policyIds must be returned by createCedarPoliciesFunction for compensation to work. alreadyExists flag skips rollback of pre-existing resources. compensateTask.addCatch(failState) guarantees terminal Fail state.

content: alb-https-tls-conditional-pattern: ChatStack ALB HTTPS uses optional certificate prop. When certificate provided: HTTP listener redirects (301) to HTTPS, HTTPS listener (port 443) uses RECOMMENDED_TLS and forwards to target group, CloudFront origin uses HTTPS_ONLY. When no certificate: HTTP forwards directly, no HTTPS listener created. ECS service depends on both listeners when HTTPS configured.

sfn-parallel-resultpath-preserves-input: Use resultPath on sfn.Parallel to preserve upstream state (e.g. tenantId) for downstream tasks. Without resultPath, Parallel output replaces state with an array and breaks $.tenantId access.

ses-receipt-rule-activation-custom-resource: SES ReceiptRuleSet must be explicitly activated via AwsCustomResource (ses:setActiveReceiptRuleSet). Use both onCreate and onUpdate handlers to avoid stale attribute errors (per cdk-aws-custom-resource-update-stale-attribute skill). onDelete should call setActiveReceiptRuleSet with empty parameters to deactivate.

email-stack-ses-s3-bucket-policy: SES requires bucket policy allowing ses.amazonaws.com to PutObject to inbound/ prefix with aws:Referer condition set to the account ID. Without this bucket policy SES silently drops emails instead of failing loudly.

grantReadWrite-does-not-exist-on-secret: aws-cdk-lib/aws-secretsmanager.Secret has no grantReadWrite() method. Use grantRead() + grantWrite() separately. Only IAM grantable resources like DynamoDB tables expose grantReadWriteData(); Secrets Manager Secret exposes grantRead()/grantWrite() only.
gh-actions-bun-version-pinning: All GH Actions workflows use BUN_VERSION: 1.2.0 and oven-sh/setup-bun@v2. Bun 1.2+ uses text-based bun.lock (not binary bun.lockb). Never use @v1 or 1.0.0.

gh-actions-release-softprops: actions/create-release@v1 and actions/upload-release-asset@v1 are archived/deprecated. Use softprops/action-gh-release@v2 with tag_name+files params instead. No upload_url needed.

gh-actions-pin-security-tools: Security scan workflow pins third-party action versions (trivy@0.29.0, trufflehog@v3.88.0, checkov@v12). Never use @master/@main for security tooling — mutable refs are a supply chain risk.

gh-actions-bun-version-pinning: Updated BUN_VERSION from 1.2.0 to 1.3.11 in all GH Actions workflows (ci.yml, security-scan.yml, deploy.yml, release.yml). Also updated Docker base image references in ADR-020 (oven/bun:1.2-alpine -> oven/bun:1.3-alpine) and research docs (oven/bun:1.0 -> oven/bun:1.3).

cdk-cross-stack-kms-sqs-lambda-cycle: When Stack A owns a KMS key used by Stack B for SQS encryption, and Stack B has Lambda event sources on those SQS queues, CDK auto-creates KMS grants in Stack A referencing Lambda role ARNs from Stack B, creating a circular dependency. Fix: create a stack-local KMS key in the consuming stack instead of passing the key cross-stack.

cdk-sqs-kms-cross-stack-grant-cycle: When SQS queues in Stack B use a KMS key from Stack A, Lambda addEventSource() auto-creates key policy grants in Stack A referencing Lambda role ARNs from Stack B — creating a circular CDK dependency. Fix: use a stack-local KMS key for SQS encryption so all grants stay within the same stack.

hatchling-single-file-wheel-include: When pyproject.toml project name (e.g. chimera-agents) normalizes to a package name (chimera_agents) that doesn't match the actual source layout (single-file chimera_agent.py), hatchling fails with 'Unable to determine which files to ship'. Fix: add [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] include list to bypass package discovery and explicitly list source files.

bun-default-export-fetch-auto-serve: Bun auto-starts an HTTP server when a module has export default with a .fetch method. In Docker (bun server.js), this fires alongside any explicit require.main === module serve() call, binding port twice and crashing. Fix: use named export only (export { app }), never export default a Hono/fetch handler.

ecr-pull-through-docker-hub-requires-credentials: ECR pull-through cache for registry-1.docker.io (Docker Hub) requires authenticated credentials. Must create Secrets Manager secret under path 'ecr-pullthroughcache/' with {username, accessToken} keys, then pass --credential-arn when calling aws ecr create-pull-through-cache-rule. Unlike public.ecr.aws, Docker Hub does not allow anonymous pull-through. Pull-through prefix for images: <account>.dkr.ecr.<region>.amazonaws.com/docker-hub/library/<image> for official images, /docker-hub/<org>/<image> for org images.

ecr-pull-through-docker-hub-requires-credentials: Direct Docker Hub pulls (FROM oven/bun:1.3-alpine, FROM python:3.11-slim) are simpler than ECR pull-through cache. ECR pull-through for registry-1.docker.io requires Docker Hub credentials pre-configured in Secrets Manager. Optional docker login via DOCKER_HUB_USERNAME/TOKEN env vars is the preferred rate-limit mitigation — set these in CodeBuild via dockerHubSecretArn prop on PipelineStack.

agent-docker-base-image: packages/agents/Dockerfile uses ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:python3.11 as base — official Astral image with Python 3.11 + uv pre-installed, no separate COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv needed. packages/chat-gateway/Dockerfile uses oven/bun:1.3-alpine — purpose-built Bun runtime, smallest Alpine image.

cli-deploy-local-to-codecommit: Use 'bun run packages/cli/src/cli.ts deploy --source local --region us-west-2' with AWS_PROFILE set to push local repo to CodeCommit; batches 665 files into 7 commits, skipping unchanged batches

chat-gateway-dockerfile-debian-bun-curl: packages/chat-gateway/Dockerfile uses debian:bookworm-slim base with bun installed via curl https://bun.sh/install instead of oven/bun:1.3-alpine — avoids Docker Hub rate limits (429) in CodeBuild

cdk-nag-adoption: CDK Nag with AwsSolutions pack adopted for all 11 stacks. Runs during cdk synth via Aspects.of(app).add(new AwsSolutionsChecks()). Known suppressions: AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole (IAM4), pinned Lambda runtimes (L1), terminal DLQs (SQS4), self-referencing access log buckets (S1). Add 'bun add -D cdk-nag' to infra package.
